questions.question_text: As of April 2025, how many hours of pre-license education must an Ohio salesperson candidate complete?
questions.explanation: HB 238 reduced Ohio's pre-license education requirement from 120 to 100 hours effective April 9, 2025. The reduction came from cutting Real Estate Finance and Appraisal from 20 hours each to 10.

questions.question_text: An Ohio salesperson candidate has completed all 100 hours of pre-license education. What must they secure before applying for the exam?
distractors: A passing grade on a state quiz
A letter from the Real Estate Commission
questions.explanation: Ohio requires salesperson candidates to be sponsored by a licensed Ohio broker before applying for the licensing exam. The broker agrees to supervise the new licensee.

questions.question_text: Under HB 238, Ohio reduced which two pre-license courses from 20 hours to 10 hours each?
distractors: Principles and Practices, and Ohio Law
Ohio Law and Real Estate Finance
questions.explanation: HB 238 reduced Real Estate Finance and Real Estate Appraisal from 20 hours to 10 hours each. Real Estate Principles and Practices (40 hours) and Ohio Real Estate Law (40 hours) remained unchanged.

questions.question_text: What is the minimum age to obtain an Ohio real estate salesperson license?
questions.explanation: Ohio requires salesperson license applicants to be at least 18 years of age. Additional requirements include education, broker sponsorship, and a background check.

questions.question_text: An Ohio salesperson candidate has completed pre-license education and passed the exam. What type of background check is required before licensure?
questions.explanation: Ohio requires a fingerprint-based background check before issuing a salesperson license. This is separate from education, exam, and broker sponsorship requirements.

questions.question_text: A newly licensed Ohio salesperson must complete how many hours of post-licensing education, and within what timeframe?
questions.explanation: Newly licensed Ohio salespersons must complete a 20-hour post-licensing course within 12 months of initial licensure. This is separate from the triennial continuing education requirement.

questions.question_text: Ohio's triennial continuing education requirement consists of how many total hours?
questions.explanation: Ohio licensees must complete 30 hours of CE every 3 years, with renewal based on the licensee's birthday. Mandatory core topics include civil rights (3 hrs), legislation (3 hrs), and ethics (3 hrs).

questions.question_text: A 72-year-old Ohio licensee is renewing their license. How many CE hours must they complete?
distractors: No hours are required at age 70+
questions.explanation: Licensees age 70 and older only need to complete the 9 mandatory core hours (3 civil rights, 3 legislation, 3 ethics) instead of the full 30 hours.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is a mandatory core continuing education course in Ohio?
questions.explanation: Ohio requires three mandatory core CE courses: civil rights law (3 hrs), current state and federal legislation (3 hrs), and ethics (3 hrs). These 9 hours are required within the 30-hour total.

questions.question_text: An Ohio broker who supervises affiliated licensees must include which additional CE course in their renewal hours?
questions.explanation: Brokers and management-level licensees must complete an additional 3-hour course on principal broker duties within their 30 hours of CE. This covers brokerage operations and supervisory responsibilities.

questions.question_text: An Ohio licensee places their license in permanently resigned status. Later they want to practice again. What must they do?
questions.correct_answer: Apply for a completely new license
distractors: Complete 30 hours of CE to reactivate
Pay a reinstatement fee to the Division
Pass only the state portion of the exam
questions.explanation: Permanently resigned status is final and cannot be reversed. The former licensee must meet all current requirements and apply for a new license, including education and examination.

questions.question_text: An Ohio salesperson has not practiced real estate in two years but wants to keep their license. What status should they request?
questions.explanation: Inactive status allows a licensee to maintain their license without practicing. No CE is required while inactive. Permanently resigned status is irreversible and should not be chosen if the licensee may return.

questions.question_text: Which statement about an inactive Ohio real estate license is correct?
questions.correct_answer: No CE is required while inactive
distractors: The licensee may still show properties
The license expires after two years
questions.explanation: Inactive licensees are not required to complete continuing education for renewal. However, they cannot perform any licensed real estate activities while in inactive status.

questions.question_text: An Ohio licensee wants to resign their license mid-term. When can they apply for permanently resigned status?
questions.correct_answer: At any time before the renewal deadline
distractors: Only at the end of the renewal period
Only after completing current CE hours
Only with Commission approval at a hearing
questions.explanation: A licensee may apply to permanently resign at any time prior to their required renewal date. The resignation is considered final without any action by the Superintendent.

questions.question_text: Which Ohio license status results from disciplinary action by the Commission?
questions.explanation: The Commission can suspend (up to 5 years) or revoke a license through formal disciplinary proceedings. Inactive and permanently resigned are voluntary statuses chosen by the licensee.

questions.question_text: A property owner rents their own single-family home without using a real estate agent. Does this require an Ohio real estate license?
questions.correct_answer: No, owners managing their own property are exempt
distractors: Yes, all rental activity requires a license
Only if the monthly rent exceeds $1,000
Only if the lease term exceeds 12 months
questions.explanation: Property owners managing their own real estate do not need a license. A license is required when performing real estate activities for others for compensation.

questions.question_text: Which activity requires an Ohio real estate license?
questions.correct_answer: Listing property for sale for another person for a fee
distractors: Giving free advice to a friend about pricing
A bank managing its own foreclosed properties
questions.explanation: Listing, selling, or leasing real property for others for compensation requires an Ohio real estate license. Personal transactions by owners and certain institutional activities are exempt.

questions.question_text: A friend asks you to help sell their house and offers to pay you a finder's fee. You do not have a real estate license. What is the consequence?
questions.correct_answer: You would be engaging in unlicensed activity
distractors: This is permitted for a one-time transaction
This is legal if the fee is under $500
This only requires registering with the county
questions.explanation: Performing real estate activities for others for compensation without a license is a violation of Ohio law. The Division can issue a cease and desist order for unlicensed activity.

questions.question_text: An attorney handles a real estate closing as part of their law practice. Does this require a separate real estate license in Ohio?
distractors: Yes, all closing work requires licensure
Only if the attorney receives a commission
questions.explanation: Attorneys performing real estate activities as part of their law practice are generally exempt from real estate licensing requirements. The exemption applies to services incidental to their legal practice.

questions.question_text: A corporation wants to sell properties it owns. Must the corporation hold a real estate license?
questions.correct_answer: No, entities selling their own property are exempt
distractors: Yes, all commercial sales require a license
Only if annual sales exceed five properties
Only if the properties are residential
questions.explanation: Owners (including corporations) selling their own real property are exempt from licensing. A license is required only when acting on behalf of others for compensation.
